phrasal_verbs.meaning: produce sth such as heat or a smell
think of (an idea, a plan, etc)
find the solution to a problem, etc

attempt: make an attempt (at sth/doing / to do)
consider: it strange, etc (for sb to do)

questions.sentence: It seems entirely (1) ...... to modern audiences that doctors conduct rigorous trials before introducing any new treatment,
yet it took centuries of dedicated effort for physicians to (2) ...... the true causes of most common diseases.
Getting to grips with the human body was certainly not a straightforward (3) ......,
and early physicians struggled greatly to understand the true (4) ...... of illness and infection.
Eventually, a growing number of physicians realised they could (5) ...... their ideas by performing systematic observations and noting the outcomes.
Setting up a controlled (6) ...... became the cornerstone of the new scientific approach to medicine.
Enormous (7) ...... was made once scientific methods began to be applied to medicine.
Major institutions began funding (8) ...... into diseases that had once been considered untreatable,
though early pioneers often received little (9) ...... for their years of dedication and sacrifice.
In time, it became (10) ...... that medicine could only advance through collaboration and shared knowledge.
She created a completely **artificial / false** identity online, including a made-up name and profile picture.
The child received an **electric / electronic** shock when he accidentally touched the exposed wire.
The mechanic told us that the **engine / motor** in the boat needed to be completely replaced.
The pharmaceutical **industry / factory** is currently investing heavily in new cancer treatments.
The international science conference is **taking place / occurring** in Vienna next month.
Solar panels have become a standard feature of **new / modern** architecture around the world.
The mechanics discovered a serious fault in the **engine / machine** of the racing car.

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verbs from the box.

questions.sentence: Our laboratory printer had ...... down, so we couldn't print the experiment results.
After hours of debate, the panel had it ...... down to three possible candidates.
Could you please ...... the heating off? It's getting far too warm in here.
Scientists are trying to ...... out why the new vaccine is less effective in elderly patients.
The biology team will be ...... out a series of experiments on plant cells all this week.
Who ...... up with the idea of using solar panels to charge small portable devices?
I'll just ...... you through to our technical support team right away. Please hold on.
The new microscope still isn't working. Are you sure you've ...... it in properly?

D.title: Matching (Phrasal verbs)
D.instructions: Match the sentence beginnings (1–6) with the correct endings (A–F).

questions.sentence: The entire neighbourhood had its power cut **A** off during the severe storm last night.
Open the windows - those heated chemicals give **E** off toxic fumes that can damage your lungs.
I'm preparing my science report and need to find **C** out who first split the atom.
The team's bold plan finally came **F** off - their prototype worked perfectly on the first attempt.
Medieval scholars spent decades trying to turn **B** base metals such as iron into silver.
Your professor says your understanding of physics has really come **D** on this term.
Scientists conducted a series of trials ...... a newly discovered strain of bacteria.
Space exploration was extremely expensive ...... the beginning of the programme.
We need to face the ...... - this experiment has not produced any useful results.
The temperature inside the laboratory is, ...... average, about twenty-two degrees Celsius.
The probe detected unusual rock formations ...... the bottom of the ocean trench.
The astronaut ...... hundreds of photographs of the Earth's surface from the space station.
After years of extensive study, the team has ...... to the conclusion that the planet may once have supported life.
Would you mind if I just ...... a quick phone call before we begin the presentation?
Fewer patients suffered from post-operative infections ...... the introduction of sterile surgical techniques.
Investigators are still working to identify the ...... of the laboratory fire.
The university is funding ongoing research ...... the long-term effects of air pollution on health.
The team built a special underwater vehicle ...... an attempt to film deep-sea creatures in their natural habitat.

F.title: Error correction (extra words)
F.instructions: Find the extra word in each line. Click it.

questions.sentence: We were discussing about the latest advances in genetic engineering during the seminar.
Many scientists were wondering it whether a cure for the disease would ever be found.
The professor explained them that the experiment required extremely precise measurements.
because engineers are intend to develop more fuel-efficient forms of aircraft engine.

G.title: Key word transformation
G.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
The researcher's error caused a significant delay in the entire project. **in**
The researcher's error ...... a significant delay in the entire project.

Dr Atherton finally succeeded in discovering the secret formula. **managed**
Dr Atherton finally ...... the secret formula.

Another name for sodium chloride is table salt. **as**
Sodium chloride ...... table salt.

Several environmental groups intend to protest outside the new factory. **plan**
Several environmental groups ...... outside the new factory.

We are thinking of appointing Dr Knight to lead the new research division. **considering**
We ...... the position of research director.

We cannot survive in deep space without specialised life support systems. **us**
It ...... to survive in deep space without specialised life support systems.
questions.answer: is impossible for us/is not possible for us

H.title: Word formation (text)
H.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

questions.sentence: SpaceView have just launched their (1) (REVOLUTION) ...... new deep-space telescope, the AstroMax 500.
It is (2) (POSSIBLE) ...... not to be impressed by its elegant (3) (APPEAR) ...... and its extraordinary zoom capability.

It may not be the ideal (4) (INTRODUCE) ...... to astronomy for complete beginners since it requires some technical knowledge, but a thorough (5) (EXPLAIN) ...... of every function can be found in the comprehensive user guide.
The AstroMax has been (6) (SCIENCE) ...... engineered to capture the clearest possible images of distant stars.
The (7) (INVENT) ...... of a unique built-in image stabiliser means there is an entire universe of (8) (DISCOVER) ...... just waiting for you.

I.title: Word formation (sentences)
I.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

questions.sentence: The old laboratory had ...... (WOOD) shelves lining the walls and a stone floor.
The scientist recorded careful ...... (OBSERVE) throughout every stage of the experiment.
...... (RESEARCH) at the institute have identified a possible link between sleep deprivation and memory loss.
She decided to study medicine and later specialise as a ...... (PSYCHOLOGY).
The tall glass ...... (BUILD) on the corner is the university's new robotics laboratory.
The scientists stressed the ...... (IMPORTANT) of repeating experiments in order to verify results.
The science museum is offering an ...... (INTRODUCE) course on space exploration this summer.
Some experts believe that certain ancient civilisations were far more ...... (TECHNOLOGY) advanced than we previously thought.
